tools.name: vale
tools.notes: Configured but NOT used in CI. RedHat style package has regex errors that prevent execution. Can be manually installed and run after fixing style configs.

tools.name: gitleaks
tools.notes: Configured but NOT used in CI. Used for secret detection.

tools.name: semgrep
tools.notes: Configured but NOT used in CI. Contains rules for Go, Python, TypeScript, YAML, and generic patterns.

linting.notes: Only link checking runs in CI. Vale, gitleaks, and semgrep are configured but not enforced.

testing.framework: Custom shell scripts for link validation
testing.notes: This is a documentation repository. No traditional unit/integration tests. The only 'test' is link validation which checks for broken HTTP/HTTPS links in rendered HTML. Link checking uses curl with 5s timeout per URL.

gating_checks.name: Check links in modified AsciiDoc files
gating_checks.notes: Finds modified .adoc files (modules, assemblies, books) and validates all HTTP/HTTPS URLs in rendered HTML. Skips URLs matching patterns in scripts/.links_ignore.

ci.notes: CI runs on push and pull_request. Only one workflow: link checking. Requires asciidoctor to build HTML, then extracts and validates URLs with curl. Runs up to 10 concurrent curl checks per file.

build.notes: Builds all root-level .adoc files to HTML in out/ directory. Creates index.html listing all books. Uses asciidoctor with -a toc=left -a doctype=book -a allow-uri-read -a sectanchors -a skip-front-matter. Published to ReadTheDocs at https://opendatahub.io/docs.

conventions.import_style: AsciiDoc include:: statements for assemblies and modules
conventions.notes: Documentation follows Red Hat modular documentation standards. Modules in modules/, assemblies in assemblies/, root .adoc files are book entry points. Uses product attributes from _artifacts/document-attributes-global.adoc.

lint_commands.notes: Vale can be installed but RedHat style has regex errors. Not used in CI anyway.

test_commands.notes: Build succeeded with exit code 0. Generated HTML output to out/ directory. Some warnings about duplicate IDs but build completes successfully.
Link checking works correctly. Validates HTTP/HTTPS URLs in rendered HTML using curl.

container_recipe.notes: This is a documentation repo. Build and link checking work. Vale linting has config issues. No traditional tests exist.

results.command: apt-get update && apt-get install -y asciidoctor curl git
results.output_snippet: asciidoctor (2.0.20-1) installed successfully along with dependencies
results.notes: Dependencies installed cleanly in Ubuntu 24.04 container

results.output_snippet: asciidoctor: WARNING: assemblies/modules/using-the-kubeflow-sdk-to-run-distributed-training-workloads.adoc: line 5: id assigned to section already in use
results.notes: Build succeeded. Some warnings about duplicate section IDs but does not fail build. Generated 40+ HTML books in out/ directory.

results.output_snippet:
Checking: README.adoc
✅ All links are valid!
results.notes: Link checking works correctly. README.adoc has no links so passed immediately.

results.output_snippet: E201 Invalid value [/root/.local/share/vale/styles/RedHat/CaseSensitiveTerms.yml:36:4]: capture group not supported; use '(?:' instead of '('
results.notes: Vale installed successfully but RedHat style package has regex syntax errors. Vale is configured but not used in CI anyway.

validation.summary: Build OK (warnings but succeeds), link checking OK, Vale has config errors (not used in CI)

gaps: No traditional unit or integration tests - this is a documentation repository
Vale prose linter is configured but has syntax errors in RedHat style package and is not used in CI
Gitleaks (secret detection) is configured but not used in CI
Semgrep (SAST) is configured but not used in CI
No automated spell checking or grammar checking in CI
Build produces warnings about duplicate section IDs that should be fixed
No validation that AsciiDoc structure follows modular documentation standards
Link checking is the only gating CI check
